entries.title: CATENA on first-peter 3:5
entries.excerpt: Outward beauty is not the real thing but rather something quite superfluous. A woman's proper beauty is obedience to her husband. The truth of this statement is made manifest by the examples of the holy women of the past, like Sarah.

entries.title: INTRODUCTORY COMMENTARY ON 1 PETER on first-peter 3:5
entries.excerpt: Women must remain holy in every state of life, whether they are married, single or celibate.

entries.title: Commentary on 1 Peter on first-peter 3:5
Commentary on 1 Peter on first-peter 3:5
entries.excerpt: "They adorn themselves." Do you see that this refers to adornment or decoration as subjection?

entries.title: Commentary on 1 Peter on first-peter 3:5
entries.excerpt: Here he calls obedience an adornment, saying: they adorned themselves by being obedient to their own husbands. Having spoken indefinitely of "holy women," he calls them daughters of Sarah either by faith or by descent. For daughters must necessarily imitate their mothers. Along with the adornment befitting Christian women, he urges them to be merciful as well, not fearing punishment from their husbands for it. This is what he means by the words: "you are not troubled by any fear." For it is natural that husbands will sometimes demand an accounting from them of expenditures.
